cloud.api.callFailedNoBody: Cloudflare API call failed (no response body)
cloud.ddns.bound: DDNS bound: {0} ({1}). Once monitoring starts it will follow this machine's public IP automatically.
cloud.ddns.eligibleOnly: DDNS can only be enabled for A / AAAA records
cloud.email.label: Account email:
cloud.intro: Connects to the Cloudflare API: lists every domain and DNS record in your account, lets you create/edit records, and binds A·AAAA records as DDNS that resident in the background follow this machine's public IP automatically.
cloud.ip.eligibleOnly: Only A / AAAA records can point to this machine's IP
cloud.ip.failed: Could not obtain this machine's public {0} address
cloud.ip.unknown: Public IP: unknown (shown after you start monitoring or click "Check now")
cloud.monitor.autoStart: Start monitoring automatically when the app launches
cloud.monitor.needBindings: No enabled DDNS bindings. Click "Bind DDNS" on a record in the list first.
cloud.monitor.needToken: Please save and verify the API Token first
cloud.net.timeout: Request timed out (check your network connection)
cloud.perms.dialogTitle: Permissions required by the Cloudflare API Token
cloud.perms.intro: When creating an API Token for DDNS, request the permissions in the table below:
cloud.perms.note: Note: this is the minimal permission set — only Zone scope is needed, no User / Account permissions. Because of that, Cloudflare's token-verify endpoint may return #1000, but this does not affect DDNS — this tool uses "can it read zones" as the validity test. Leave the remaining items (Client IP Address Filtering, TTL) blank.
cloud.perms.path: Cloudflare dashboard → Profile → API Tokens → Create Token → use the "Custom token" template
cloud.perms.rowResourcesDesc: Or select only the specific domains you want to use for DDNS.
cloud.perms.rowZoneDnsDesc: Read and create / edit DNS records — the core DDNS permission.
cloud.perms.rowZoneReadDesc: List every domain (Zone) in your account.
cloud.record.contentLabel: Content (A/AAAA: IP; CNAME: target domain; TXT: text)
cloud.record.nameLabel: Name (subdomain; use @ for the root; resolves to *.{0})
cloud.record.proxyCheckbox: Proxy through Cloudflare (orange cloud; hides origin IP)
cloud.record.ttlLabel: TTL (forced to "Auto" when Cloudflare proxy is on)
cloud.results.zonesEmpty: No domains found (make sure the token has the Zone:Read permission)
cloud.section.boundDesc: The A / AAAA records below update automatically with this machine's public IP (use the checkbox to pause one individually).
cloud.section.boundEmpty: No records bound yet. Click "Bind DDNS" on an A / AAAA record under "Domains & DNS records" above.
cloud.section.pauseTooltip: Enable / pause automatic updates for this record
cloud.token.intro: We recommend a Cloudflare "API Token" (Profile → API Tokens → Create Token) with the Zone:Zone:Read and Zone:DNS:Edit permissions. You can also use a "Global API Key" + account email. Credentials are encrypted with Windows DPAPI and stored on this machine only — never checked into the repository.
cloud.token.invalidEmptyKey: Please enter the Global API Key
cloud.token.invalidHint1000:
Cloudflare rejected this credential (the request was well-formed; the problem is the credential value itself). Please check:
① Use an "API Token" (Profile → API Tokens → Create Token) with the Zone:Zone:Read + Zone:DNS:Edit permissions.
② The token is complete with no extra spaces/newlines (invisible characters are stripped automatically) and belongs to the same account.
③ If you only have a "Global API Key", switch to "Global API Key" mode and enter your account email.
cloud.token.invalidHint6003:
#6003 means the request headers were not accepted — the credential type does not match the mode selected above:
→ If you are using an "API Token": switch the mode to "API Token" (no email needed) and retry.
→ If you are using a "Global API Key": it should be 37 hexadecimal characters (Profile → API Tokens → Global API Key → View) and you must enter the correct account email.
cloud.token.invalidHint9103: The Global API Key or account email is incorrect. Verify it under "Profile → API Tokens → Global API Key".
cloud.token.needEmail: Global API Key mode requires an account email
cloud.token.statusUsable: ✓ Credential usable (the token-verify endpoint did not pass, but {0} domains were read successfully, so DDNS works fine)
cloud.zones.needVerify: Please enter and verify a credential first
